steps.title: Identify the grammatical structures on each side of the blank
steps.content:
Before the blank: an independent clause—the sculptures are meant to challenge viewers to consider their own relationships to material (subject: sculptures, verb: are meant).
After the blank: an independent clause—her thought provoking works in the 1993 exhibition Steel Clothes and Ornamentation at the Redfern Gallery in London were no different (subject: works, verb: were).

steps.title: Apply the rule for joining independent clauses
steps.content:
A semicolon joins two independent clauses, and option A uses this punctuation to correctly separate the two clauses.

Option C creates a comma splice, option D creates a run-on, and option B introduces “and to,” which doesn't work because “her works were no different” is an independent clause with its own subject and verb, not an object phrase to follow “to consider.” That matches choice A — A is correct.
